subject: Human Resources
title: Pay Transparency Laws
description: A 60-second lesson on Pay Transparency Laws.

hook.text: Colorado passed a law requiring salary ranges on job postings. Some companies responded by excluding Colorado from remote roles.

buildup.text: Pay transparency laws require employers to disclose salary ranges in postings, during hiring, or to current employees.

discovery.text: Studies show transparency reduces pay gaps—when inequity is visible, companies are pressured to fix it.

twist.text: Posting wide ranges—like $60K-$180K—technically complies but provides no useful information. The spirit of the law matters.

climax.text: Transparency is expanding globally—the EU, several U.S. states, and Canada are all moving toward mandatory disclosure.

punchline.text: Hiding pay was the old normal. Showing it is the new one.

quiz.question: What effect does pay transparency have on pay gaps?
options: It pressures companies to reduce inequity
